subject: english
topic_title: Punctuation Marks and Words Associated with the Press
learning_objectives: Identify and use commas, semicolons, question marks, and colons correctly
Define and use vocabulary associated with journalism and the press

sections.heading: Punctuation Marks
sections.content: Punctuation marks are signs used to mark off words or groups of words for effective presentation. 1. The comma ( , ) is used to mark off words in a list or separate clauses. 2. The semicolon ( ; ) is used to separate main clauses which are not linked by a conjunction. 3. The question mark ( ? ) is used at the end of a direct question. 4. The colon ( : ) is used to introduce a list or to provide additional information.

sections.heading: Vocabulary: Words Associated with the Press
sections.content: 1. Tabloid: a newspaper with small pages. 2. Verbatim: a word-for-word report. 3. Journalism: the task of collecting and writing news for newspapers. 4. Editorial: the task of preparing a newspaper or magazine. 5. Headline: the title of a news item in large letters. 6. Byline: the name of the person who wrote the article.
